title: Stock not decremented after confirmed payment
description: When an order is confirmed as 'paid' (via payment_result or webhook), neither products.in_stock nor products_variants.stock is reduced. This causes inventory to drift immediately after the first sale.
implementation_notes: In payment_result() and webhook() after status='settled' is confirmed, iterate over products_orders_items for that order_id. If product has active variants, decrement products_variants.stock for the matching variant; otherwise decrement products.in_stock. Floor at 0 — do not fail the order if stock would go negative.
acceptance_criteria: Note product in_stock before placing order
Complete full checkout and EveryPay demo payment
Verify in_stock in DB is reduced by the ordered quantity after confirmation

title: Cart quantity not capped to available stock
description: add_to_cart() and update_cart() write quantities to $_SESSION['cart'] without checking in_stock. A shopper can add more items than physically available.
implementation_notes: In add_to_cart() (line ~140) and update_cart() (line ~159), query in_stock (or products_variants.stock if variant is selected) and cap the session quantity. If stock = 0, reject the add and return an error message.
acceptance_criteria: Set a product's in_stock to 2
Attempt to add 5 units to cart
Verify cart shows quantity 2, not 5
Verify add-to-cart button is disabled when in_stock = 0

title: Webhook does not reliably confirm payment when shopper closes browser
description: payment_result() only runs when the shopper is redirected back from EveryPay. If the browser is closed before redirect, the order stays 'pending' forever. webhook() exists but it is unclear if it fully updates order status, decrements stock, and triggers email.
implementation_notes: Audit webhook() (line ~443). It must: (1) verify EveryPay signature/status, (2) update products_orders.status to 'paid', (3) call the same stock-decrement logic as SHOP-001, (4) send confirmation email (SHOP-005). Extract shared logic into a private _confirm_order($order_id) method called by both payment_result() and webhook().
acceptance_criteria: Complete payment on EveryPay demo
Do NOT follow redirect back — close browser
Simulate EveryPay webhook POST to /products/webhook
Verify order status is 'paid' in DB
Verify stock decremented and email sent

title: Discount price ignored in cart and checkout totals
description: products.discount_price column exists and is saved via admin, but cart, checkout, and order creation logic may be using the full price field instead of discount_price when it is set. Shoppers are charged the wrong amount.
implementation_notes: Wherever a line total is calculated use: (discount_price > 0) ? discount_price : price. Applies to: checkout() total calculation (~line 288), cart.php display, cart_panel.php display. The price stored in products_orders_items must be the actual charged price (discount_price when set).
acceptance_criteria: Create a product with price=50 and discount_price=30
Add to cart — cart should show 30
Proceed to checkout — total should reflect 30
Complete order — products_orders_items.price should store 30

title: Broken category routes — deleted view files
description: Views beach.php, naujos.php, products.php, surf.php are deleted (shown as D in git status). The controller still has index(), naujos(), surf(), beach() methods that reference them. These routes return errors.
implementation_notes: Replace the four dead methods with a single category($slug) method that: (1) queries products_categories table for the slug, (2) 404s if not found, (3) calls _listing($slug, $category->name). All existing category links should point to /products/category/{slug}. listing.php already exists and should be reused.
acceptance_criteria: Navigate to /products/category/surf — page loads with surf products
Navigate to /products/category/beach — page loads with beach products
Navigate to /products/category/invalid — returns 404
Old routes /products/surf, /products/beach no longer throw errors

title: Admin endpoints missing security checks
description: Admin methods create(), submit(), submit_delete(), show(), and manage() should all call $this->trongate_security->_make_sure_allowed() before executing. Without this, unauthenticated users can access admin routes directly.
implementation_notes: Add $this->trongate_security->_make_sure_allowed() as the first line of each admin method. Reference the Events module controller for the correct pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Log out of admin
Navigate directly to /products/create
Verify redirect to login — not the create form
Same check for /products/manage, /products/show/{id}, /products/submit

title: No admin order list or order detail view
description: There is no way for admin staff to view placed orders. products_orders and products_orders_items exist in the DB but are not exposed in the admin panel.
implementation_notes: Add orders() method: paginated table of products_orders using existing get_limit() / get_offset() / reduce_rows() helpers. Columns: ID, customer name, email, total, delivery, status, date. Add show_order($id) method: shows customer info + line items table from products_orders_items joined with products. Add corresponding view files: orders.php, show_order.php.
acceptance_criteria: Admin navigates to /products/orders
Sees paginated list of all orders with status
Clicks an order — sees line items, customer info, delivery method
Can filter by status (pending / paid / failed)

title: No order confirmation email sent to customer
description: After payment is confirmed, the customer receives no email. Brevo API is already configured in config/config.php (BREVO_API key present).
implementation_notes: In the shared _confirm_order($order_id) method (see SHOP-003), after updating order status to 'paid', POST to Brevo transactional email API. Email should contain: order ID, item list with quantities and prices, total, delivery method, and a thank-you message. Use the customer email from products_orders.
acceptance_criteria: Complete a test order with a real email address
Verify email is received within 2 minutes
Email contains order ID, items, total, delivery info

title: Omniva parcel locker cache has no TTL — stale locker data
description: _get_omniva_lockers() writes omniva_cache.json on first call but never refreshes it. Parcel locker locations and names change; shoppers may see outdated options.
implementation_notes: In _get_omniva_lockers(), before returning cached data check filemtime of omniva_cache.json. If file is older than 86400 seconds (24 hours), re-fetch from Omniva API and overwrite the cache. If the API request fails and cache exists (even stale), return the stale cache. If no cache and API fails, return empty array and show a notice in checkout.
acceptance_criteria: Set cache file mtime to 25 hours ago
Load checkout page — verify fresh data is fetched from Omniva API
Block Omniva API — verify checkout still loads using stale cache

title: Variant stock vs product stock — ambiguous source of truth
description: products.in_stock and products_variants.stock are separate columns. It is undefined which is authoritative when a product has variants. Cart capping (SHOP-002) and stock decrement (SHOP-001) will behave incorrectly without a clear rule.
implementation_notes: Establish and enforce: if a product has at least one active variant (products_variants where is_active=1), use products_variants.stock per variant as the source of truth. If no active variants, use products.in_stock. Apply this rule consistently in add_to_cart(), update_cart(), and stock decrement logic.
acceptance_criteria: Product with variants: add size M to cart — qty capped to products_variants.stock for size M
Product without variants: qty capped to products.in_stock
After order confirmed: correct stock column is decremented

title: Price change between cart add and checkout not communicated to shopper
description: If a product price changes after a shopper adds it to cart, checkout() silently charges the new price with no notice. Shopper sees a different total than expected.
implementation_notes: In checkout(), after fetching current DB prices, compare against any price stored in the session (if tracked) or simply display a notice: 'Prices have been updated since you added items to your cart.' Pass a $price_changed flag to the checkout view to conditionally render the notice.
acceptance_criteria: Add product to cart at price 40
Change product price to 45 in admin
Load checkout page — verify a price-changed notice is shown
Total reflects the new price 45

title: EveryPay credentials stored in version-controlled config file
description: EVERYPAY_API_USERNAME and EVERYPAY_AUTH_KEY are committed to git in config/config.php. Currently demo keys, but the same file will hold production keys. If the repo is ever public or cloned, credentials are exposed.
implementation_notes: Move sensitive keys (EVERYPAY_AUTH_KEY, BREVO_API, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) to a server environment variable or a gitignored secrets file (e.g. config/secrets.php included by config.php but listed in .gitignore). Replace hardcoded values with getenv() calls.
acceptance_criteria: config/config.php committed to git contains no secret keys
Application still functions — keys loaded from environment or gitignored file
secrets file documented in README

title: No error handling if EveryPay API is unreachable during checkout
description: _get_everypay_link() uses raw curl_exec with no timeout or error check. If EveryPay is down, process_order() will silently fail or hang, leaving the shopper on a blank screen with an order created but no payment link.
implementation_notes: Add CURLOPT_TIMEOUT (10s) and CURLOPT_CONNECTTIMEOUT (5s) to the curl request. Check curl_errno() and http_response_code. If the request fails, do not insert the order — or mark it 'payment_failed' — and redirect back to checkout with a flash error: 'Payment service unavailable. Please try again.'
acceptance_criteria: Simulate EveryPay API unavailability (block the URL or use wrong endpoint)
Submit checkout form
Verify user is redirected back to checkout with an error message
Verify no orphan order record is left in pending state

title: Controller too large — mix of admin, storefront, cart, and payment in one file
description: Products.php is 1114 lines covering admin CRUD, public product listing, cart management, checkout, and payment integration. This makes the file hard to navigate and increases the risk of unintended side-effects when editing.
implementation_notes: Extract DB queries into a Products_model.php (or use a separate Orders.php module for order management). At minimum, extract _get_everypay_link(), _get_omniva_lockers(), and the new _confirm_order() into private helper methods or a service file. Admin order methods (SHOP-007) could live in a separate Orders controller.
acceptance_criteria: All existing routes continue to function after extraction
Main controller is under 600 lines
No logic duplication between files

title: Wishlist lost on cookie clear — no persistent storage for logged-in scenario
description: Wishlist is stored in a 30-day cookie, making it device-specific and easily lost. The trongate_users module exists but wishlist is not tied to any user account.
implementation_notes: For now, document the limitation. Future improvement: if user is authenticated, store wishlist in a products_wishlist (user_id, product_id) DB table and sync with cookie on login.
acceptance_criteria: Wishlist cookie behaviour documented
No regression in current cookie wishlist behaviour
